title: Terra sigillata burnishing on stoneware
notes: Applying terra sigillata (a highly deflocculated refined-clay slip) to bone-dry or very leather-hard stoneware, then burnishing with a smooth stone or back of a spoon to build up a sheen before bisque firing. The sheen survives a single-fire to low earthenware temperature (cone 06/04) but is partly lost at cone 6. Discusses the tradeoff between burnished surface and functional stoneware glazing. Different from the air-dry version (terra-sigillata-burnishing-air-dry) in that this version is kiln-fired.
